content.content:
# Installing Docker Engine on Ubuntu

## Prerequisites

Before installation, ensure your system meets these requirements:

- **OS**: 64-bit Ubuntu (Resolute 26.04 LTS, Questing 25.10, Noble 24.04 LTS, or Jammy 22.04 LTS)
- **Architecture**: x86_64, armhf, arm64, s390x, or ppc64le compatible
- **Firewall awareness**: Docker exposes container ports that may bypass firewall rules; `nft` rules aren't supported
- **Remove conflicts**: Uninstall any unofficial Docker packages (`docker.io`, `docker-compose`, `podman-docker`, etc.) and previous `containerd`/`runc` installations

## Official Installation via APT Repository

The recommended approach involves these steps:

1. **Set up Docker's repository** by adding GPG keys and configuring apt sources with Docker's official repository URL

2. **Install packages** using: `"sudo apt install docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin"`

3. **Verify installation** by running the hello-world image: `"sudo docker run hello-world"`

The Docker service starts automatically post-installation. Users requiring non-root access should consult post-installation Linux configuration steps.

## Alternative Methods

- **Manual package installation**: Download `.deb` files directly and install via `dpkg`
- **Convenience script**: Use `https://get.docker.com/` for development environments (not production-recommended)

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Update package manager
apt-get update

# Install prerequisites
apt-get install -y \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    gnupg \
    lsb-release

# Add Docker's official GPG key
install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg

# Set up the Docker repository
echo \
  "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu \
  $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" | \
  tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null

# Update package manager again
apt-get update

# Install Docker packages
apt-get install -y \
    docker-ce \
    docker-ce-cli \
    containerd.io \
    docker-buildx-plugin \
    docker-compose-plugin

# Start Docker daemon
systemctl start docker || service docker start || true

# Enable Docker on boot
systemctl enable docker || true

# Verify installation
docker --version
echo "Docker installed successfully"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Start Docker daemon if not already running
sudo service docker start || true

# Wait for Docker to be ready
for i in {1..30}; do
    if docker ps &>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ Docker daemon is ready"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "✗ Docker daemon failed to start"
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Launch Docker CLI with a simple info command to show it's running
docker info
echo ""
echo "✓ Docker is running successfully"

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Start Docker daemon
echo "Starting Docker daemon..."
sudo nohup dockerd > /tmp/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
DAEMON_PID=$!
echo "Docker daemon PID: $DAEMON_PID"

# Wait for Docker socket to be created and daemon ready
echo "Waiting for Docker daemon to be ready..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if [ -S /var/run/docker.sock ]; then
        echo "✓ Docker socket found"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "✗ Docker daemon socket not found"
        cat /tmp/dockerd.log
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Wait for daemon to respond
for i in {1..30}; do
    if sudo docker ps &>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ Docker daemon is ready"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "✗ Docker daemon failed to respond"
        cat /tmp/dockerd.log
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Display Docker info
echo ""
echo "Docker version info:"
docker --version
echo ""
echo "Docker system info:"
sudo docker info
echo ""
echo "✓ Docker is running successfully"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Add current user to docker group
echo "Adding user to docker group..."
sudo usermod -aG docker $(whoami)

# Start Docker daemon if not already running
echo "Starting Docker daemon..."
if ! pgrep -x "dockerd" > /dev/null; then
    sudo nohup dockerd > /tmp/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
    DAEMON_PID=$!
    echo "Docker daemon PID: $DAEMON_PID"
    
    # Wait for Docker socket to be created
    echo "Waiting for Docker socket..."
    for i in {1..30}; do
        if [ -S /var/run/docker.sock ]; then
            echo "✓ Docker socket found"
            break
        fi
        if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
            echo "✗ Docker daemon socket not found"
            cat /tmp/dockerd.log 2>/dev/null || true
            exit 1
        fi
        sleep 1
    done
    
    # Small delay for daemon to fully initialize
    sleep 2
else
    echo "Docker daemon already running"
fi

# Test the connection
echo "Testing Docker connection..."
if sudo docker ps &>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ Docker connection successful with sudo"
    DOCKER_CMD="sudo docker"
else
    echo "Cannot connect with sudo, trying without..."
    exit 1
fi

# Display Docker info
echo ""
echo "Docker version info:"
$DOCKER_CMD --version
echo ""
echo "Docker system info:"
$DOCKER_CMD info
echo ""
echo "✓ Docker is running successfully"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Start Docker daemon as root if not already running
echo "Starting Docker daemon..."
if ! sudo pgrep -x "dockerd" > /dev/null; then
    sudo nohup dockerd > /tmp/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
    DAEMON_PID=$!
    echo "Docker daemon PID: $DAEMON_PID"
    
    # Wait for Docker socket to be created
    echo "Waiting for Docker socket..."
    for i in {1..30}; do
        if [ -S /var/run/docker.sock ]; then
            echo "✓ Docker socket found"
            break
        fi

#!/bin/bash

# Find Docker binary path
BINARY_PATH=$(which docker || command -v docker)
BINARY_NAME="docker"

# Get version
VERSION=$(docker --version | grep -oP 'Docker version \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')

# Find display name
DISPLAY_NAME="Docker"

# Find .desktop file (if it exists)
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop"
fi

# Find icon files
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -f "/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg")
fi

# If no icons found, look for any docker-related icons
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    FOUND_ICONS=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "*docker*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -n "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done <<< "$FOUND_ICONS"
fi

# Create JSON output
JSON=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $([ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && echo "\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" || echo "null"),
  "icon_paths": [$(printf '"%s",' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sed 's/,$//')]$([ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ] && echo "[]" || echo ""),
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF
)

# Output JSON
echo "$JSON"

input.name: linux-e91d9013
input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Find Docker binary path
BINARY_PATH=$(which docker || command -v docker)
BINARY_NAME="docker"

# Get version
VERSION=$(docker --version | grep -oP 'Docker version \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')

# Find display name
DISPLAY_NAME="Docker"

# Find .desktop file (if it exists)
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop"
fi

# Find icon files
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -f "/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg")
fi

# If no icons found, look for any docker-related icons
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    FOUND_ICONS=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "*docker*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -n "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done <<< "$FOUND_ICONS"
fi

# Build icon array JSON
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON},"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}]"

# Build desktop entry JSON
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_JSON="null"
fi

# Create JSON output
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Kill any existing dockerd processes
sudo pkill -9 dockerd || true
sleep 1

# Start Docker daemon with simplified options for sandbox environment
echo "Starting Docker daemon..."
sudo nohup dockerd --iptables=false --ip-forward=false --bridge=none > /tmp/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
DAEMON_PID=$!
echo "Docker daemon PID: $DAEMON_PID"

# Wait for Docker socket to be created
echo "Waiting for Docker socket..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if [ -S /var/run/docker.sock ]; then
        echo "✓ Docker socket found"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "✗ Docker daemon socket not found"
        tail -20 /tmp/dockerd.log 2>/dev/null || true
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Wait for daemon to respond
echo "Waiting for Docker daemon to be responsive..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if sudo docker ps &>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ Docker daemon is responsive"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "✗ Docker daemon not responding"
        tail -30 /tmp/dockerd.log 2>/dev/null || true
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Display Docker info
echo ""
echo "=== Docker Version ==="
sudo docker --version
echo ""
echo "=== Docker System Info ==="
sudo docker info
echo ""
echo "✓ Docker is running successfully"

#!/bin/bash

# Find Docker binary path
BINARY_PATH=$(which docker || command -v docker)
BINARY_NAME="docker"

# Get version
VERSION=$(docker --version | grep -oP 'Docker version \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')

# Find display name
DISPLAY_NAME="Docker"

# Find .desktop file (if it exists)
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/docker.desktop"
fi

# Find icon files
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -f "/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/docker.svg")
fi

# If no icons found, look for any docker-related icons
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    FOUND_ICONS=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "*docker*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -n "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done <<< "$FOUND_ICONS"
fi

# Build icon array JSON
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON},"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}]"

# Build desktop entry JSON
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_JSON="null"
fi

# Create JSON output
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

# Find Docker binary path
BINARY_PATH=$(which docker || command -v docker)
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    echo '{"error": "Docker binary not found"}' >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Derive binary name from the full path
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get version from the binary
VERSION=$(docker --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'version \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Initialize arrays and variables
DISPLAY_NAME=""
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
ICON_PATHS=()

# Try to find and parse .desktop files to extract display name
if [ -d "/usr/share/applications" ]; then
    DESKTOP_FILES=$(find /usr/share/applications -name "*docker*" -type f 2>/dev/null)
    for desktop_file in $DESKTOP_FILES; do
        if grep -q "^Name=" "$desktop_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Extract Name field from desktop file
            NAME_FROM_DESKTOP=$(grep "^Name=" "$desktop_file" | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$NAME_FROM_DESKTOP" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$NAME_FROM_DESKTOP"
                DESKTOP_ENTRY="$desktop_file"
                break
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Fallback to capitalize binary name if no desktop file found
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$BINARY_NAME" | sed 's/^./\U&/')
fi

# Use package manager to find all installed files for docker
if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
    # For Debian/Ubuntu systems
    PKG_FILES=$(dpkg -L docker-ce 2>/dev/null || dpkg -L docker 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    # Search for .desktop files in package files
    DESKTOP_FROM_PKG=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep "\.desktop$" | head -1)
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG"
        # Extract name from the desktop file if we didn't get it before
        if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ] || [ "$DISPLAY_NAME" = "Docker" ]; then
            PARSED_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$PARSED_NAME" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PARSED_NAME"
            fi
        fi
    fi
    
    # Find icons from package files
    ICON_FILES=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico)$" | grep -i docker)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_FILES"
elif command -v rpm &>/dev/null; then
    # For RedHat/CentOS systems
    PKG_FILES=$(rpm -ql docker 2>/dev/null || rpm -ql docker-ce 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    DESKTOP_FROM_PKG=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep "\.desktop$" | head -1)
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG"
        if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ] || [ "$DISPLAY_NAME" = "Docker" ]; then
            PARSED_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$PARSED_NAME" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PARSED_NAME"
            fi
        fi
    fi
    
    ICON_FILES=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico)$" | grep -i docker)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_FILES"
fi

# Also search standard icon directories
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    ICON_SEARCH=$(find /usr/share/icons -type f \( -name "*docker*" -o -iname "*docker*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            # Avoid duplicates
            if ! printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | grep -q "^$icon_file$"; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
            fi
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_SEARCH"
fi

if [ -d "/usr/share/pixmaps" ]; then
    ICON_SEARCH=$(find /usr/share/pixmaps -type f \( -name "*docker*" -o -iname "*docker*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            if ! printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | grep -q "^$icon_file$"; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
            fi
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_SEARCH"
fi

# Set default display name if still not found
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="Docker"
fi

# Build desktop entry JSON
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_JSON="null"
fi

# Build icon paths JSON array
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON},"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}]"

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

VERSION=$(docker --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'version \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Initialize arrays and variables
DISPLAY_NAME=""
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
ICON_PATHS=()

# Try to find and parse .desktop files to extract display name
if [ -d "/usr/share/applications" ]; then
    DESKTOP_FILES=$(find /usr/share/applications -name "*docker*" -type f 2>/dev/null)
    for desktop_file in $DESKTOP_FILES; do
        if grep -q "^Name=" "$desktop_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Extract Name field from desktop file
            NAME_FROM_DESKTOP=$(grep "^Name=" "$desktop_file" | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$NAME_FROM_DESKTOP" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$NAME_FROM_DESKTOP"
                DESKTOP_ENTRY="$desktop_file"
                break
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Fallback to capitalize binary name if no desktop file found
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$BINARY_NAME" | sed 's/^./\U&/')
fi

# Use package manager to find all installed files for docker
if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
    # For Debian/Ubuntu systems
    PKG_FILES=$(dpkg -L docker-ce 2>/dev/null || dpkg -L docker 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    # Search for .desktop files in package files
    DESKTOP_FROM_PKG=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep "\.desktop$" | head -1)
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG"
        # Extract name from the desktop file if we didn't get it before
        if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ] || [ "$DISPLAY_NAME" = "Docker" ]; then
            PARSED_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$PARSED_NAME" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PARSED_NAME"
            fi
        fi
    fi
    
    # Find icons from package files
    ICON_FILES=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico)$" | grep -i docker)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_FILES"
elif command -v rpm &>/dev/null; then
    # For RedHat/CentOS systems
    PKG_FILES=$(rpm -ql docker 2>/dev/null || rpm -ql docker-ce 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    DESKTOP_FROM_PKG=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep "\.desktop$" | head -1)
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG"
        if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ] || [ "$DISPLAY_NAME" = "Docker" ]; then
            PARSED_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FROM_PKG" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/^Name=//')
            if [ -n "$PARSED_NAME" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PARSED_NAME"
            fi
        fi
    fi
    
    ICON_FILES=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico)$" | grep -i docker)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_FILES"
fi

# Also search standard icon directories
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    ICON_SEARCH=$(find /usr/share/icons -type f \( -name "*docker*" -o -iname "*docker*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            # Avoid duplicates
            if ! printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | grep -q "^$icon_file$"; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
            fi
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_SEARCH"
fi

if [ -d "/usr/share/pixmaps" ]; then
    ICON_SEARCH=$(find /usr/share/pixmaps -type f \( -name "*docker*" -o -iname "*docker*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            if ! printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | grep -q "^$icon_file$"; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
            fi
        fi
    done <<< "$ICON_SEARCH"
fi

# Set default display name if still not found
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="Docker"
fi

# Build desktop entry JSON
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_JSON="null"
fi

# Build icon paths JSON array
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON},"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON}]"

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF
